test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 1500.001s.
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/aks_byo_node.go:175 with:
Expected
    <int32>: 3
to equal
    <int32>: 5
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1500.001s.
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/aks_byo_node.go:175 with:
Expected
    <int32>: 3
to equal
    <int32>: 5
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/aks_byo_node.go:183 @ 06/08/26 17:02:26.599

ai_summary.summary: AKS cluster node pool update conflict caused test timeout

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failure occurred because an attempt to update the AKS cluster was made while a node pool upgrade operation was already in progress. The Azure Activity Log shows a 'Failed' event with status 'Conflict' and message 'Operation is not allowed because there's an in progress upgrade node pool operation (operation ID: 3ccb4ae7-4fd2-45c2-9657-92d111179fdc) on the agent pool pool2 started on UTC 2026-06-08T16:36:00Z. Please wait for it to finish before starting a new operation.' This conflict caused the test to timeout after 1500.001s as it was waiting for the expected number of nodes (5) but only 3 were ready. The MachinePool resources show all three pools (pool0, pool1, pool2) were eventually ready with 1 replica each, but the conflicting operations prevented the test from reaching its expected state within the timeout period.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Modify the test to ensure that cluster update operations are not initiated while node pool upgrade operations are in progress. This can be achieved by adding checks to wait for any existing operations to complete before starting new ones. Specifically, in the test code around aks_byo_node.go:175, implement a polling mechanism to verify the cluster and node pools are in a stable state before proceeding with further operations.
